How to Grab QueryString Params in JavaScript (a Few Different Ways)

Sometimes you need to pull QueryString params out of a URL with JavaScript. This post is basically a note to myself on the different ways to do it — from the old-school, IE-friendly approach using split(), to the newer URL and URLSearchParams APIs. I'll also cover what to do when you hit encoded Chinese characters in the URL.

XSS Attacks: The Frontend Security Bug You Really Should Know About
Security
XSS

Understanding XSS attacks is one of those things every frontend developer really should know. So here are my notes on what XSS actually is — the three main types (stored, reflected, and DOM-based), and a couple of simple ways to defend against it on the frontend, including why `createElement` can be safer than `innerHTML`.

Building FreeCell Solitaire in JavaScript: The Tricky Bits Nobody Warns You About

I've been digging into how to build FreeCell-style Solitaire in JavaScript, and there are a bunch of small tricky bits worth writing down. This post collects the directions and hints I figured out — drag and drop (both jQuery UI and vanilla JS), generating random numbers, shuffling cards into columns, and the extra conditions a real Solitaire game needs.
/en/javascript/2019/06/27/javascript-ajax-vanilla/
AJAX in Vanilla JavaScript: XMLHttpRequest, Fetch, and a jQuery Bonus

A quick note-to-self on how to do AJAX in plain JavaScript — the old-school XMLHttpRequest way, the newer Fetch API, and a bonus jQuery $.ajax example. Along the way I jot down the gotchas I ran into, like Callback Hell and Fetch not working in Internet Explorer 11.
/en/javascript/2019/06/25/javascript-let-const-hoisting-tdz/
The let and const Hoisting Gotcha: Why It's ReferenceError, Not undefined

I ran into a JavaScript quiz that I was sure would log undefined & undefined — and it didn't. Turns out let and const do get hoisted, but they behave differently from var thanks to something called the Temporal Dead Zone (TDZ). This post records the question, the answer, and what's actually going on.
